Russian TST 20193
Day 6	May 7, 2019
P1	Unavailable
P2	Prove that for every odd prime number $p{}$, the following congruence holds
\[\sum_{n=1}^{p-1}n^{p-1}\equiv (p-1)!+p\pmod{p^2}.\]

oVlad
view topic
P3	Find the maximal value of
\[S = \sqrt[3]{\frac{a}{b+7}} + \sqrt[3]{\frac{b}{c+7}} + \sqrt[3]{\frac{c}{d+7}} + \sqrt[3]{\frac{d}{a+7}},\]
where $a$, $b$, $c$, $d$ are nonnegative real numbers which satisfy $a+b+c+d = 100$.

Proposed by Evan Chen, Taiwan

62861
view topic
